citation_1_locator: Forest-sector overview: Namibia lacks a formal industrial plantation base or rich commercially exploited natural forests; the resource base is semi-open woodlands, savannahs, and deserts under arid conditions.

capacity_note: Full Lourenço Marques includes Maputo, Maputo City, Inhambane, Gaza, and southern Sofala/Manica; the capacity arithmetic carries no frozen south-of-Pungwe commercial-hectare split, so the public cap is supplied only by the separate one-cap exception.

citation_1_locator: Table 4 / p. 20: forest plantation area is 149 ha in Maputo and 0 ha in Gaza, versus much larger plantation figures in provinces such as Sofala.

citation_2_locator: Plantations section: Maputo and Gaza plantations were public dune-fixation and eucalypt plantings, while the bulk of plantations concentrated in Manica and Sofala.
validation_note: Corrected full-state scope invalidates the Maputo-Gaza-only exclusion; exact south-of-Pungwe Manica/Sofala commercial forestry hectares are not frozen; the cap is handled by adjustment-input-0261.
